title: What would I invest in if I knew it would work?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for surfacing investing beliefs and the fear of getting it wrong.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for examining the beliefs that keep investing on the to-do list rather than in motion, and what would change if the fear of getting it wrong lifted.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you know investing is the next step but something keeps stopping you from starting.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. The prompts move from naming the block to imagining what action looks like when the fear of getting it wrong is out of the way.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What is the story I tell myself about why I haven't started investing yet?
Write it plainly. Is it about money, knowledge, timing, or something else?
What is the fear underneath that story?
Write the fear directly: "I'm afraid that if I invest, I will..."
If I knew it would work, what would I invest in first?
Write the answer. Don't evaluate it for correctness — just write what comes.
What is the smallest real step I could take toward investing this week?
Write it as a specific action, not a category: open an account, read one article, transfer a set amount.
What would I tell a friend who had the same story about investing not being for them?
Write the advice. Then write whether you are willing to take it yourself.
Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
